title: Additive queue overlays require deterministic materialization with field-level provenance and retained conflicts
summary: The repository now contains a non-authoritative reducer for ACTIVE_QUEUE.json plus additive queue-update documents. It orders overlays by explicit event_order, then observed_at, then lexical source path; preserves provenance for every materialized field; refuses cross-owner mutation; and retains incompatible same-order writes as conflicts instead of silently selecting a winner.

status_relations.observed: Reducer and deterministic test source are committed on main.
status_relations.inferred: A generated queue view can now be reproducible without becoming a new authoritative store.
status_relations.proposed: Add event_order and observed_at to future queue-update records and run the reducer in CI to retain a generated view plus source map.
status_relations.superseded: The unresolved portion of CM-1003 stating that no committed reducer was identified is superseded by this implementation; CM-1003's historical observation remains valid at its observation time.
status_relations.unresolved: No GitHub Actions run exists for the reducer test commit, so test execution and corpus-wide compatibility are not yet proven. Older queue-update files may also lack explicit event_order and observed_at fields.

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch commits 411cd34450b6026402b6a95e13edf3a4f9345770 and 5b2169fc900a9d4729fb46dc56b4e75dd57fe516. Falsify the implementation claim if the reducer does not preserve field-level source metadata, allows owner changes, silently overwrites incompatible same-order fields, or emits authoritative=true. Execute the committed Node tests and a repository-wide corpus run before promoting the generated view into CI evidence.
